assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing Security Configuration section with Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, and Source Repositories unchanged
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 51-73 contain the complete '## Security Configuration' section with all three subsections: '### Product Lifecycle' (5 fields: Product pages URL, Jira version prefix MYPRODUCT, Vulnerability issue type ID 10200, Component label pattern pscomponent:, VEX Justification custom field customfield_12345), '### Version Streams' (table with 2.1.x stream), and '### Source Repositories' (table with backend and frontend-ui entries). All values match the expected original configuration.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md reports that Security Configuration is already fully configured or up to date
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 43-49 state under '### Security Configuration': '**Status**: Already fully configured' and enumerate all subsections as present: 'Product Lifecycle: All 5 fields present', 'Version Streams: 1 stream configured (2.1.x)', 'Source Repositories: 2 repositories configured (backend, frontend-ui)', concluding with 'No changes needed'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates Security Configuration was preserved with no changes needed
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md line 15 shows Security Configuration status as 'Fully configured (Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, Source Repositories) | Preserved as-is'. Lines 23-27 detail: 'Security Configuration: Preserved all subsections unchanged. Opt-in prompt not shown — section already fully populated.' with itemized preservation of all subsections. Line 30 states 'No modifications made. Output is a faithful copy of the existing configuration.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT offer the Security Configuration opt-in prompt — it detects the section already exists and is fully populated (idempotency skip)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 48 states 'Opt-in prompt skipped — section already fully populated' under Security Configuration. changes-log.md line 23 states 'Opt-in prompt not shown — section already fully populated.' Both files confirm the opt-in prompt was skipped due to idempotency detection.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Repository Registry entries (backend and frontend-ui) unchanged
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 14-17 show the Repository Registry table with two entries: '| backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/backend |' and '| frontend-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/frontend-ui |'. Both entries are preserved with their original values.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with all three fields (Bug issue type ID, Bug template, Bug-to-Task link type) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 45-49 show '## Bug Configuration' with all three fields: 'Bug issue type ID: 10001', 'Bug template: docs/bug-template.md', 'Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks'. discovery-log.md lines 36-40 report Bug Configuration as 'Already configured' with 'All required fields present' and 'No changes needed'. changes-log.md line 22 confirms 'Preserved all fields (Bug issue type ID: 10001, Bug template: docs/bug-template.md, Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks) unchanged'.
